document.title: Biological evaluation of medical devices — Part 5: Tests for in vitro cytotoxicity

sourceSummary.oneLine: ISO 10993-5:2009 specifies in vitro cytotoxicity test methods for biological evaluation of medical devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to assess in vitro cytotoxicity of medical devices by exposing cultured mammalian cells to devices or device extracts directly or through diffusion.
keyPoints: The standard lists three test categories: extract test, direct contact test, and indirect contact test.
Sample preparation shall be in accordance with ISO 10993-12.
Negative and positive controls shall be included in each assay.
Quantitative evaluation of cytotoxicity is preferable, while qualitative means are appropriate for screening.
Reduction of cell viability by more than 30 percent is considered a cytotoxic effect.
Cytotoxicity data shall be assessed in relation to other biocompatibility data and the intended use of the product.

entities.name: ISO
entities.evidence: ISO (the International Organization for Standardization) is a worldwide federation of national standards bodies (ISO member bodies).

entities.name: ISO/TC 194
entities.evidence: ISO 10993-5 was prepared by Technical Committee ISO/TC 194, Biological evaluation of medical devices.

entities.name: IEC
entities.evidence: ISO collaborates closely with the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) on all matters of electrotechnical standardization.

entities.name: Medical devices
entities.evidence: This part of ISO 10993 describes test methods to assess the in vitro cytotoxicity of medical devices.

entities.name: Mammalian cells
entities.evidence: These methods are designed to determine the biological response of mammalian cells in vitro using appropriate biological parameters.

concepts.name: In vitro cytotoxicity
concepts.evidence: This part of ISO 10993 describes test methods to assess the in vitro cytotoxicity of medical devices.

concepts.name: Extract test
concepts.evidence: Three categories of test are listed: extract test, direct contact test, indirect contact test.

concepts.name: Direct contact test
concepts.evidence: Three categories of test are listed: extract test, direct contact test, indirect contact test.

concepts.name: Indirect contact test
concepts.evidence: Three categories of test are listed: extract test, direct contact test, indirect contact test.

concepts.name: Sample and control preparation
concepts.evidence: Sample preparation shall be in accordance with ISO 10993-12. Negative and positive controls shall be included in each assay.

concepts.name: Determination of cytotoxicity
concepts.evidence: Determine cytotoxic effects by either qualitative or quantitative means. Quantitative evaluation of cytotoxicity is preferable.

concepts.name: Neutral red uptake cytotoxicity test
concepts.evidence: Annex A (informative) Neutral red uptake (NRU) cytotoxicity test

concepts.name: Colony formation cytotoxicity test
concepts.evidence: Annex B (informative) Colony formation cytotoxicity test

evidence.claim: The document is ISO 10993-5:2009(E), third edition, dated 2009-06-01.
ISO 10993-5:2009 is an International Standard for biological evaluation of medical devices, Part 5, tests for in vitro cytotoxicity.
evidence.evidenceText: INTERNATIONAL STANDARD ISO 10993-5:2009(E) ... Biological evaluation of medical devices — Part 5: Tests for in vitro cytotoxicity

evidence.claim: The scope is to describe test methods to assess in vitro cytotoxicity of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This part of ISO 10993 describes test methods to assess the in vitro cytotoxicity of medical devices.

evidence.claim: The methods specify incubation of cultured cells in contact with a device or device extracts directly or through diffusion.
evidence.evidenceText: These methods specify the incubation of cultured cells in contact with a device and/or extracts of a device either directly or through diffusion.

evidence.claim: ISO 10993-1 and ISO 10993-12 are normative references.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 10993-1, Biological evaluation of medical devices — Part 1: Evaluation and testing within a risk management system; ISO 10993-12, Biological evaluation of medical devices — Part 12: Sample preparation and reference materials

evidence.claim: The standard lists extract, direct contact, and indirect contact tests as cytotoxicity test categories.
evidence.evidenceText: Three categories of test are listed: extract test, direct contact test, indirect contact test.

evidence.claim: The choice of test category depends on the sample nature, potential site of use, and nature of use.
evidence.evidenceText: The choice of one or more of these categories depends upon the nature of the sample to be evaluated, the potential site of use and the nature of the use.

evidence.claim: The test shall be performed on an extract of the test sample and/or the test sample itself.
evidence.evidenceText: The test shall be performed on a) an extract of the test sample and/or b) the test sample itself.
Sample preparation shall be in accordance with ISO 10993-12.

evidence.claim: Negative and positive controls are required in each assay.
evidence.evidenceText: Negative and positive controls shall be included in each assay.

evidence.claim: Culture medium with serum is the preferred extraction vehicle.
evidence.evidenceText: Culture medium with serum is the preferred extraction vehicle.

evidence.claim: A minimum of three replicates shall be used for test samples and controls.
evidence.evidenceText: A minimum of three replicates shall be used for test samples and controls.

evidence.claim: Qualitative and quantitative methods may be used to determine cytotoxicity, with quantitative evaluation preferred.
evidence.evidenceText: Determine cytotoxic effects by either qualitative or quantitative means. Quantitative evaluation of cytotoxicity is preferable. Qualitative means are appropriate for screening purposes.

evidence.claim: A numerical grade greater than 2 based on Tables 1 and 2 is considered a cytotoxic effect.
evidence.evidenceText: The achievement of a numerical grade greater than 2, based on Tables 1 and 2, is considered a cytotoxic effect.

evidence.claim: Reduction of cell viability by more than 30 percent is considered a cytotoxic effect.
evidence.evidenceText: Reduction of cell viability by more than 30 % is considered a cytotoxic effect.

evidence.claim: The test report must include testing facility, personnel, test dates, sample description, cell line, assay method, extraction procedure, controls, cell response, and relevant assessment data.
evidence.evidenceText: The test report shall include at least the following details: a) name and address of testing facility; b) name of the person(s) who conducted the test; c) dates of start and end of the test; d) description of the sample; e) cell line, justification of the choice and cell source(s); f) name of company and batch of medium, serum and antibiotics, when added; g) assay method and rationale; h) extraction procedure ... i) negative, positive and other controls; j) cell response and other observations; k) any other relevant data necessary for the assessment of results.

evidence.claim: Overall assessment shall be performed by a person capable of making informed decisions based on the test data.
evidence.evidenceText: The overall assessment of the results shall be carried out by a person capable of making informed decisions based on the test data.

evidence.claim: Cytotoxicity data shall be assessed with other biocompatibility data and the intended use of the product.
evidence.evidenceText: Cytotoxicity data shall be assessed in relation to other biocompatibility data and the intended use of the product.

evidence.claim: A cytotoxic effect may indicate potential in vivo toxicity, but the device cannot necessarily be deemed unsuitable based solely on cytotoxicity data.
evidence.evidenceText: Any cytotoxic effect can be of concern. However, it is primarily an indication of potential for in vivo toxicity and the device cannot necessarily be determined to be unsuitable for a given clinical application based solely on cytotoxicity data.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text identifies the document as an ISO International Standard prepared by ISO/TC 194; FDA authority is not evidenced in the source text.
No product model is identified; productSignal is Unclassified and the source concerns medical devices generally.
The text contains repeated copyright and license artifacts from the preprocessed PDF, but the main standard content is readable.
